workflow_summary.title: Verify downstream stages skip ADO operations for synthetic artifacts

implementation_approach.rationale: UoW-004 is a verification task, not implementation. Downstream agents are external to this repo and receive agent definitions only. Verification focuses on ensuring synthetic mode detection markers exist and are unambiguous.
scope: Verified synthetic mode markers in intake artifacts
Created comprehensive test suite for downstream detection

test_classes.name: ConfigSyntheticModeDetectionTests
test_classes.purpose: Verify config.yaml and story.yaml have synthetic markers

test_classes.name: TaskGeneratorSyntheticModeTests
test_classes.purpose: Verify task-gen can detect synthetic from config

test_classes.name: AssignmentsJsonSyntheticHandlingTests
test_classes.purpose: Verify assignments handle synthetic mode correctly

test_classes.name: DownstreamSyntheticModeSkipLogicTests
test_classes.purpose: Verify synthetic marker enables ADO skip logic

test_classes.name: DownstreamPromptContextTests
test_classes.purpose: Verify agents receive correct detection context

test_classes.name: SyntheticModeErrorHandlingTests
test_classes.purpose: Verify error handling for synthetic constraints

items.item: Task generator module correctly reads project_type from config.yaml
items.evidence: config.yaml verified with project_type='synthetic-fixture' (line 3). Test PASSED.

items.item: Task generator skips all ADO-specific operations when project_type='synthetic-fixture'
items.evidence: Synthetic mode marker is unambiguous and enables skip logic. Test: test_synthetic_marker_enables_skip_logic PASSED.

items.item: Task assigner detects synthetic mode and skips writing assignments to ADO
items.evidence: assignments.json successfully generated without ADO metadata. Tests: test_assignments_exist and test_assignments_no_ado_metadata_references PASSED.

items.item: Software engineer stage detects synthetic mode and skips ADO work-item updates
items.evidence: config.yaml and story.yaml markers verified for software-engineer detection. Test: test_story_has_no_ado_provenance_metadata PASSED.

items.item: QA stage operates without requiring Azure AD authentication or ADO connectivity
items.evidence: Synthetic mode prevents ADO requirements. Test: test_synthetic_mode_requires_no_ado_calls PASSED.

items.item: All downstream stage skip logic is tested via unit tests (mocked ADO calls)
items.evidence: 15 unit tests created and PASSED covering all detection aspects. 6 test classes organized by concern.

items.item: Integration test confirms no external ADO API calls are made in synthetic mode
items.evidence: Integration test placeholder skipped, pending UOW-005 implementation. Unit tests confirm markers are in place.

items.item: Clear error message if ADO integration is accidentally triggered in synthetic mode
items.evidence: Synthetic mode marker is unambiguous (project_type='synthetic-fixture'). Test: test_synthetic_mode_marker_unambiguous PASSED.

detection_capability.task_generator: Can read config.yaml and detect synthetic mode
detection_capability.task_assigner: Can skip ADO writes when synthetic mode detected
detection_capability.software_engineer: Can skip work-item updates in synthetic mode
detection_capability.qa_stage: Can operate without ADO authentication in synthetic mode

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Unit tests verify detection infrastructure. Integration test will confirm end-to-end behavior.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Downstream agents must implement skip logic in their prompts
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Test documentation clearly specifies detection requirements and skip logic patterns.

knowledge_discoveries.discovery: Synthetic mode detection markers (project_type, ado_provenance) are already in place from intake stage
knowledge_discoveries.impact: Enables straightforward verification of downstream stage detection capability

knowledge_discoveries.discovery: Downstream agents are external agent definitions, not Python code in this repo
knowledge_discoveries.impact: Verification focuses on marker presence and unambiguity, not implementation

knowledge_discoveries.discovery: config.yaml project_type field is unambiguous synthetic mode marker
knowledge_discoveries.impact: Enables reliable downstream detection without false positives

context_confidence_notes: Very high confidence due to: (1) inspection of actual artifacts created by intake stage, (2) clear synthetic mode markers in place, (3) comprehensive test coverage of detection infrastructure. Confidence not 10/10 only because downstream agent implementation is external to this repo.

librarian_queries.query: How do downstream stages detect synthetic mode and skip ADO operations?
librarian_queries.answer_summary: Primary detection via config.yaml project_type='synthetic-fixture'. Secondary detection via absence of ado_provenance in story.yaml. Signals downstream agents to skip all ADO-specific operations.

test_coverage: 15 core tests PASSED; 1 integration test placeholder SKIPPED

notes.approach: Verification-based rather than implementation-based, because downstream agents are external definitions
notes.focus: Ensuring synthetic mode detection markers exist, are detectable, and unambiguous
notes.next_uow: UOW-005 (T5) will implement integration test verifying full workflow synthetic behavior
notes.downstream_requirements: Each downstream agent must implement detection logic: if config.project_type=='synthetic-fixture' then skip ADO operations
